title: amaranthus coesius
intro: Amaranthus coesius, also known as Coesius pigweed, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. Amaranthus coesius has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Amaranthus coesius include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
